name: Cyprus

colors.symbolism: peace between the Greek and Turkish Cypriot communities
the copper for which Cyprus has been famed since antiquity (the island's name is the etymological source of 'copper'); rendered in a copper-yellow shade
the olive branches of peace and the island's natural greenery

description: A white field charged with a copper-coloured silhouette map of the island of Cyprus, beneath which two green olive branches are crossed at the stems.
history: Adopted on 16 August 1960 with the independence of Cyprus from the United Kingdom. The design was selected from a competition by the first president, Archbishop Makarios III, and the Turkish Cypriot vice-president Fazıl Küçük; it was deliberately required to avoid the colours and symbols of either Greece (blue/white cross) or Turkey (red/crescent) so as to be neutral between the two communities. The proportions were tightened and the colours specified more precisely on 24 April 2006.

features.role: outline of the island of Cyprus
features.symbolism: the territory of the whole island; the copper colour recalls the island's ancient association with copper

features.role: two crossed olive branches beneath the map
features.symbolism: peace and reconciliation between the island's Greek and Turkish communities
